name: Fieldpoint Private Bank & Trust
description_short: Private bank offering comprehensive wealth management, commercial banking, and personal banking services with FDIC-insured deposits and newly launched credit cards.
description_long:
Fieldpoint Private Bank & Trust is a private banking institution that positions itself as a client-advocate financial services provider. The company operates with a philosophy of sitting "on the same side of the table" with clients to help create, strengthen, and sustain wealth. Their service model emphasizes personalized attention and modern technology to simplify financial management for high-net-worth individuals and businesses.

Fieldpoint Private offers a comprehensive suite of banking products including personal checking, savings, CDs, money markets, online banking and bill pay, residential mortgages, securities-backed loans, and FDIC insurance protection into the tens of millions. On the commercial side, they provide acquisition/development/construction loans, working capital loans, term loans, capital call lines of credit, letters of credit, and custom credit strategies. They also offer trust services, wealth transfer planning, delegated and directed trusts, and have recently launched personal, purchasing, and business credit cards issued jointly with Pinnacle Bank (a Tennessee bank).

The company distinguishes itself through emphasis on modern technology paired with personalized service, positioning private banking as "frictionless" and focused on returning clients their most valuable resource: time. They highlight industry-leading products and state-of-the-art service, and specifically market QSBS (Qualified Small Business Stock) tax planning strategies to entrepreneurs and founders. Their credit cards offer rewards programs and purchasing categories with cashback structures.

As a private bank, Fieldpoint serves a specific market segment of affluent individuals and successful businesses rather than mass-market consumers. The website emphasizes relationship banking and custom solutions but provides limited specifics on APRs, fees, or minimum account requirements. The company's focus on private banking means accessibility and account minimums are likely substantial, making it unsuitable for average consumers. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking accounts with online banking and bill pay
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) and money market accounts
Residential mortgages for personal banking clients
Working capital loans and term loans for businesses
Capital call lines of credit for investment funds
Letters of credit and custom credit strategies
Trust services including delegated and directed trusts
Wealth transfer planning and legacy planning services
Personal and business credit cards with rewards programs
Treasury cash management and operating accounts with online management
pros: FDIC insurance protection extended into the tens of millions, significantly above standard limits
Comprehensive suite of services including personal banking, commercial banking, trust services, and wealth planning all under one relationship
Recently launched proprietary credit cards with rewards and purchase categories to unlock time and benefits for clients
Specialized tax planning services including QSBS strategies designed for entrepreneurs and startup founders
Securities-backed loans and capital call lines of credit available for high-net-worth clients
Modern online banking and treasury management tools including bill pay and operating accounts
Delegated and directed trust services with emphasis on custom wealth transfer planning and legacy continuity
cons: Private banking model with unstated (likely substantial) minimum account balances and net worth requirements
Limited transparency on fees, interest rates, APRs, and specific account terms on public website
Credit card rewards program details are vague; redemption options and earning rates vary by merchant category codes which merchant classification may not guarantee
Regional focus as Tennessee-based bank may limit accessibility for consumers outside primary service areas
No mention of mobile banking app capabilities or specific digital banking features beyond online banking and bill pay
best_for: High-net-worth individuals seeking consolidated private banking relationships with trust and wealth planning services
Entrepreneurs and business founders looking for both personal banking and commercial financing with QSBS tax planning expertise
Commercial real estate investors and businesses needing construction loans, acquisition financing, and securities-backed credit solutions
Families with substantial assets seeking delegated and directed trust services and multi-generational wealth transfer planning
diagnosis: Fieldpoint Private Bank & Trust is designed exclusively for affluent individuals, families with significant assets, and successful businesses seeking consolidated private banking relationships with specialized wealth management and trust services. The primary caveat is that this is not a bank for average consumers—account minimums, net worth requirements, and the private banking model mean most typical banking customers would not qualify or find accessible pricing.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
